commentary: Nine activities versus twelve last week, which sounds like slacking until you remember that last week you were basically a machine with a gym bag and this week you still got five swims in, including three consecutive days of two-a-days that would make a lesser man weep into his protein shake. The run on Saturday was your big cardio moment and at 13.1km with a heart rate of 151 it was working hard, which tracks, but the real story is those morning swims holding steady at 2000 metres across three separate sessions with your heart rate actually dropping over the week — 149 on Sunday, 137 on Monday, 134 on Wednesday — which means your body is getting better at this and you should be very smug about that. Last week's swim volume was slightly higher thanks to the B&B session clocking 1528 metres compared to this week's 1364, but the consistency of your long sessions is rock solid and the double-session days show you are absolutely not coasting. The one thing worth eyeing is the gap on Friday — no activity logged, which is fine, rest is not a character flaw, but two consecutive rest days to close the week suggests your body might have been asking nicely for a break after all that mid-week intensity, and honestly you should listen to it occasionally.
